Sidebar link: Introduction Sidebar link: Installation Sidebar link: Configuration

Configuration Reference

The configuration file controls how the service starts up. Every key is optional; sensible defaults apply when a key is omitted. The most commonly changed setting is the listen address, shown in the example below.

Minimal example

[server]
host = "0.0.0.0"
port = 8080
workers = 4

The workers value should usually match the number of CPU cores available to the process. Setting it far higher wastes memory on idle worker state without improving throughput.

Environment overrides

Any file key can be overridden by an environment variable using the APP_ prefix. Environment values always win over the file so that container deployments can stay immutable while still being tunable.

Example Docs. Edit this page on GitHub.
